Storage_Post_1.TutorialText_11_F5F98E9C47DA80DC659CA998:
NSLOCTEXT("", "7242265B407F285D8B739BAA6395532C", "Maeve can only carry a certain amount of <Important>Weight</> at one time, made up of all the items currently carried in the <Important>Inventory</>.

Different items have different <Important>Weights</>, so pay attention when gathering resources!

Hovering over an item shows its <Important>Tooltip</>, which shows the <Important>individual weight</> of one item, and the amount of that item in the <Important>'stack'</>.

The top-right of the <Important>Inventory</> panel shows Maeve's current vs maximum carryable <Important>Weight</>.

You won't be able to pick up new items when Maeve has reached her maximum <Important>Weight</> limit.

You will be able to obtain <Important>Upgrades</> to increase Maeve's maximum carrying <Important>Weight</> later.

In the mean-time, you can utilize <Important>Storage Containers</>.")

Storage_Post_2.TutorialText_11_F5F98E9C47DA80DC659CA998: NSLOCTEXT("", "28FB00B24DAEA7DF72E2A39EEFC53878", "Storage Containers like this newly-built Makeshift Crate allow you to store items in your Habitats for later use. Different Storage Containers can hold different amounts of items, based on their total Weight. You can see the maximum held Weight of the Facility in its Info Panel. You will be able to unlock larger Storage Containers as you progress through the game. You can deposit items into a Storage Containers by Interacting with it like any other Facility, then using (LMB) or (RMB) on the item's icon in your Inventory, to transfer one item or the whole stack, respectively. The Info Panel of the destination must be open in order to deposit items. Take items from a Storage Containers in the same way, by using (LMB) or (RMB) on the desired item in the Inventory section of the Info Panel of the Facility. One special aspect of Storage Containers is that items within them will automatically be available for use in Crafting, alongside any items in your own Inventory.")
